title: Unholy Tales of Horror & Woe From The Imperium
notes: Collection/anthology row present; individual contents not explicitly recoverable from TLBranson list.

curation.synopsis: An omnibus collecting four full-length horror novels from the grim-future imprint. J C Stearns' The Oubliette opens with a young noblewoman dragged into a sealed-off oubliette of a forgotten subsector and the maddening creature waiting for her there. Nick Kyme's Sepulturum follows a medicae in a hive that is going wrong from the inside out as the dead refuse to stay dead. David Annandale's The Deacon of Wounds plunges a parched, plague-touched cardinal-world into a slow rot of body and faith. Justin D Hill's The Bookkeeper's Skull centres on an Adeptus Administratum auditor who finds a heretical reliquary buried in his stack of dusty ledgers. Four novels, four corners of the same idea: that the quieter horrors of the grim-dark future are the ones that walk in unannounced.

flags.note: Roster marks 'anthology' but Unholy is an omnibus of four full-length novels (The Oubliette by J C Stearns, Sepulturum by Nick Kyme, The Deacon of Wounds by David Annandale, The Bookkeeper's Skull by Justin D Hill), not a collection of short stories
